h1: Conformal Cooling: Higher-Quality Parts, Faster Injection Molding Cycle Times

headings.text: Conformal Cooling: Higher-Quality Parts, Faster Injection Molding Cycle Times
5 Design Tips for Injection-Molded Parts With Complex Features

meta_description: Discover the benefits of incorporating conformal cooling channels into your injection mold tooling designs.

sections.heading: Conformal Cooling: Higher-Quality Parts, Faster Injection Molding Cycle Times
sections.text: Conformal Cooling: Higher-Quality Parts, Faster Injection Molding Cycle Times Published on March 23, 2023 A common challenge when designing parts for injection molding is factoring in cooling rates for distinct areas within a part’s design. In order for the mold to eject the part properly, of course it must be cooled. However, with high-volume or geometrically-complex parts, certain areas of the part may cool at different rates, resulting in longer cycle times and the potential for warpage. To avoid these issues and increase the productivity of the mold, parts can be designed with integrated conformal cooling channels that will allow water to access the deeper areas within the part, providi...
